question: Use of friend functions. Add two more functions in class Distance: 1. friend istream &operator>>( istream &input, Distance &D ) 2. friend ostream &operator<<( ostream &output, const Distance &D ). In these two functions you have to Input and Output the Distance object.

test_cases.expected:
Enter distance 1:
Enter feet: Enter inches: Enter distance 2:
Enter feet: Enter inches: Distance 1: 5 feet 8 inches
Distance 2: 3 feet 6 inches
After ++d1: 6 feet 9 inches
d3 = -d2: -3 feet -6 inches

driver: int main() { Distance d1, d2; cout << "Enter distance 1:" << endl; cin >> d1; cout << "Enter distance 2:" << endl; cin >> d2; cout << "Distance 1: " << d1 << endl; cout << "Distance 2: " << d2 << endl; ++d1; cout << "After ++d1: " << d1 << endl; Distance d3 = -d2; cout << "d3 = -d2: " << d3 << endl; return 0; }
errors: Attempting to use << operator for bit shifting on Distance object which is not defined
